_note: Round-15 follow-up #3: three holes in slot_attr_rewrite's shadow-stack discipline. (a) ForOf/ForIn left-binding never collected — the body's `row` references inside `for (const row of items)` wrongly rewrote to the template local from `{#each rows as row}`. (b) FunctionDeclaration name not hoisted — `function row() {}` followed by `row()` rewrote to `(undefined as any as Row)()`. (c) `var` was treated as block-scoped after R14 #5 added block-snapshot/truncate — the truncate popped a function-scoped `var row` declared in an inner block, and the trailing reference rewrote to the template local. Producer here exercises all three patterns; pre-fix each fired a distinct error (TS2339 for (a)'s `Row.length`, TS2349 for (b)'s `Row()` invocation, TS2322 for (c)'s `Row` → `number` assignment). Post-fix: ForOf/ForIn collect the let/const left-binding into shadowed at loop entry; `var` decls and inner FunctionDeclaration names are hoisted at function-body entry via `collect_hoisted_for_function_body`; the walker's VarDecl arm skips `var` (already hoisted). Consumer's `let:a let:b let:c` all resolve to `number`; `takesNumber(...)` passes for all three.
